negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No audited repo implements SchedulerContract construction with an externally supplied Arc<Mutex<SchedulerServiceState>> for sharing one scheduler service-state instance.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-scheduled-block-building/src/lib.rs contains compute_obligations_from_calls and explicitly documents that it replaces the old SchedulerServiceState-specific helper with a plain iterator, so the old shared service-state construction surface is absent.

notes: Confirmed gap for an old scheduling service construction invariant; the port has a replacement iterator helper but no shared service-state object.
rationale: The shared-state constructor is independent construction plumbing needed when multiple scheduler components must observe the same SchedulerServiceState. No later packet candidate supersedes it.
statement: SchedulerContract must support construction with an externally supplied Arc<Mutex<SchedulerServiceState>> so scheduler components can share one service-state instance.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct SchedulerContract with new_with_state using a shared Arc<Mutex<SchedulerServiceState>>, mutate the state through another Arc clone, and assert the contract observes the same mutation.
summary: The shared SchedulerContract/SchedulerServiceState construction surface is absent from the v2 port and dependency repos.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Attempt to construct SchedulerContract::new_with_state with a shared Arc<Mutex<SchedulerServiceState>> and observe mutations through another Arc clone; the required types/constructor are absent from the audited repo set.
title: Scheduler monitor can share state
